# Lucky Machines AutoLoop Worker

The worker is an off-chain controller process that discovers registered AutoLoop contracts and executes `progressLoop()` when ready.

## Canonical Files

- `README.md` - setup, configuration, and command reference
- `scripts/worker.js` - main polling and execution loop
- `scripts/register-controller.js` - controller registration helper
- `scripts/progress-loop.js` - one-off manual progression
- `scripts/health.js` - HTTP health endpoint server
- `scripts/logger.js` - structured JSON logging
- `scripts/runtime-config.js` - environment variable and config resolution
- `controller.config.json` - network and allowlist/blocklist (optional when NETWORK env var is set)
- `deployments.json` - deployed contract addresses by network
- `Dockerfile` - container build with health check
- `railway.toml` - Railway deployment configuration

## Required Environment

- `PRIVATE_KEY` or network-specific keys (`PRIVATE_KEY_ANVIL`, `PRIVATE_KEY_SEPOLIA`, ...)
- `RPC_URL` or network-specific RPC URLs
- `NETWORK` (optional) - overrides controller.config.json network setting
- `PORT` (optional, default 3000) - health server port

## Quickstart Commands

1. `npm install`
2. `npm run register-controller` (costs 0.001 ETH)
3. `npm start`

For cloud: `npm run cloud-start` (register + start in one command)

## Health Endpoint

GET /health returns JSON: { status, uptime, network, blockNumber, loopsMonitored, lastCheck }

## VRF Notes

- Worker checks ERC-165 support to identify VRF-compatible contracts.
- For VRF contracts, worker submits a proof envelope expected by `AutoLoopVRFCompatible`.
- Controller public key must be registered on-chain before proof acceptance.

## Related Repositories

- `../autoloop` - protocol contracts and VRF verifier
- `../autoloop-dashboard-v2` - managed service dashboard (Next.js)
